finding: Cloudflare or verification interstitial blocked two live collection sources after a prior Cloudflare smoke-test stop.
recommendation: Classify these no-submit runs as Cloudflare blocked, not failed submit attempts; keep live collection paused for blocked sources until real job detail pages are visible in the logged-in CDP profile.
proposed_change: Add a weekly audit gate that tags repeated Cloudflare or verification interstitials as a source-level platform blocker before any submit authorization review.

finding: Recent inspected packages repeatedly failed submit authorization because expected value, client quality, competition, risk, or fixed-price review gates were not satisfied.
recommendation: Keep submit gates unchanged; classify these no-submit runs as no high-quality opportunity or form gate failed based on the concrete form blockers instead of relaxing thresholds.
proposed_change: Require weekly audit summaries to separate submit-quality blockers from platform blockers and preserve zero-spend status when no submit was attempted.
evidence: sessions/2026-05-10-acquisition-os-live-test-2026-05-10T15-27-23-497Z.md: five inspected packages safe_to_submit false with expected value, competition, client quality, risk, or fixed-price manual-review blockers

finding: One live smoke run ended with the runner error budgetValue is not defined before form observations were produced.
recommendation: Classify this run as form gate failed/runtime error; require runner syntax and no-submit smoke validation after runner changes before treating later live results as policy evidence.
proposed_change: Add runtime-error as an explicit weekly blocker subtype so code failures are not mixed with market-quality or Connects blockers.

finding: Repeated Raw CDP submit attempts reported submit click did not verify success until the manual completion identified missing fixed-price milestone fields, project duration, and the fixed-price acknowledgement dialog.
recommendation: Classify unverified submit clicks as form gate failed, not market-quality failures; require fixed-price milestone description, due date, milestone amount, duration, and acknowledgement confirmation before treating a submit-authorized fixed-price package as executable.
proposed_change: Add fixed-price confirmation completeness as a submit execution checkpoint for delegated submit runs, with package-scoped continuation only when every visible required milestone field and acknowledgement state is observed.
evidence: data/runs.jsonl: multiple 2026-05-12 to 2026-05-14 acquisition runs include blocker submit click did not verify success
sessions/2026-05-14-upwork-manual-browser-submit-2026-05-13T17-03-56Z.md: fixed-price milestone fields and duration were empty or incomplete before successful submission
sessions/2026-05-14-upwork-manual-browser-submit-2026-05-13T17-03-56Z.md: success page confirmed proposal 2054608075344539649 after filling fields and acknowledgement

finding: Latest balance evidence is 15 Connects, while recent packages repeatedly showed Connects balance missing, payment or purchase button, or costs above visible balance such as 18, 19, and 20 Connects.
recommendation: Keep the reserve floor at 0 but stop before any purchase or Buy Connects UI; classify these no-submit cases as Connects unavailable or purchase gate, and prioritize packages with observed cost at or below the latest visible balance.
proposed_change: Weekly audits should separate cost-above-visible-balance and purchase-wall blockers from no-high-quality-opportunity blockers so Connects constraints do not hide otherwise useful category signals.
evidence: sessions/2026-05-15-acquisition-os-live-test-2026-05-15T01-11-41-635Z.md: packages at 14 and 20 Connects had Connects balance missing or payment or purchase button
sessions/2026-05-17-acquisition-os-live-test-2026-05-17T01-12-36-803Z.md: packages at 18 and 19 Connects had Connects balance missing and payment or purchase button

finding: After one successful proposal, later acquisition runs found jobs and built packages but repeatedly stopped on client quality, competition, risk, and submit score thresholds rather than lack of automation capacity.
recommendation: Keep submit thresholds unchanged and tighten collection/scoring preference toward target categories with stronger client quality evidence before form inspection, especially when observed Connects cost is 10 or higher.
proposed_change: Add a weekly targeting note that high-cost AI, RAG, FastAPI, and MVP opportunities need stronger payment, spend, hire history, scope clarity, and low or moderate competition before consuming proposal-form inspection slots.
evidence: sessions/2026-05-15-acquisition-os-live-test-2026-05-15T01-11-41-635Z.md: 5 packages built, 0 submitted, blockers include client quality, competition, risk, and submit score
sessions/2026-05-17-acquisition-os-live-test-2026-05-17T01-12-36-803Z.md: 5 packages built, 0 submitted, same quality and risk blocker pattern
data/runs.jsonl: audited acquisition runs since the prior weekly audit found 205 jobs and 130 proposal packages but only 1 submitted outcome

finding: Cloudflare or verification interstitials still appear as source-level blockers, including n8n-automation on 2026-05-17 after earlier fastapi-python-backend source blocks.
recommendation: Classify source interstitials as Cloudflare blocked and do not count them as failed submit or low-quality jobs; rerun only after logged-in source pages show real job content.
proposed_change: Keep a source-level platform-blocker bucket in weekly summaries, with blocked source name and session evidence, before evaluating submit authorization quality for that source.
